latest.note: Camino Inglés (Ferrol + A Coruña starts combined); 5.7% of 531,000 total Compostelas

trend.note: Record year, +7.6% YoY; UK starts +58%, Irish starts +33%

annualPilgrims.sourceNotes: All counts sourced from the Solvitur Ambulando JSON API which mirrors and extends the Oficina del Peregrino official Compostela data. The Inglés trend grew slowly until ~2014 when international interest accelerated; the route is now the fastest-growing major Camino by percentage. See docs/data-sources.md for the refresh procedure.

demographics.topNationalitiesNote: Per-route 2024 figures from Solvitur Ambulando. Note the disproportionate Italian and UK/Irish presence compared to other Caminos — Italians are overrepresented across all Caminos but the Inglés specifically attracts more UK and Irish pilgrims because of the historical English/Irish maritime origins of the route.

startingPoints.name: Ferrol
startingPoints.note: The most common modern start. ~123 km to Santiago — comfortably above the 100 km Compostela minimum. About 96% of pilgrims start here.

startingPoints.name: A Coruña
startingPoints.note: The historically older starting point. ~75 km to Santiago — below the 100 km minimum. Since December 2016, A Coruña pilgrims can qualify for the Compostela by walking an additional 25 km on another connected Camino.

startingPointsNote: Per-starting-point breakdown is published by Oficina del Peregrino but not exposed in the Solvitur API at the per-route level. The split is approximately 96% Ferrol / 4% A Coruña in modern years.

growthAndTrend.en: The Camino Inglés is the fastest-growing major Camino by percentage in recent years. From a low base of just 267 pilgrims in 2003, the route has grown to over 30,000 in 2025. Year-over-year growth in 2025 was +7.64% overall, with disproportionate growth from countries with historical maritime connections to the route: UK starts up 58% YoY, Irish starts up 33%.

comparisonWithOthers.en: At ~123 km, the Inglés is the shortest of the major Caminos and the easiest to fit into a single week of vacation. Among 2025 Compostelas, the Inglés ranks 4th by volume (5.7%) behind Frances (45.6%), Portugués Central (19.0%), and Portugués Coastal (16.9%).

sources.name: Solvitur Ambulando — Yearly Demographics API
sources.notes: Primary source for 2003-2025 per-route counts and demographics. Mirrors and extends Oficina del Peregrino data with per-route age, gender, motivation, and nationality breakdowns. See docs/data-sources.md.

sources.name: Camino Inglés Galicia (official)
sources.notes: Official Galician tourism board article confirming 2025 record + UK/Irish growth percentages.

sources.name: Wikipedia: English Way
sources.notes: Historical context, route description, A Coruña / 100 km rule explanation.
